$comment: Structured reference for the 25 predefined procedures of the DonorPerfect Online XML API (v7.1, 2024.02.16). Source: DPO_SUP_Manual_XML_API_Documentation050324.pdf. Each procedure is invoked as action=<name> at https://www.donorperfect.net/prod/xmlrequest.asp with named parameters in &params=.

dp_donorsearch.purpose: Search for donors by demographic criteria. Use % as wildcard. NULL fields = match anything.
dp_donorsearch.returns: List of matching donor records (donor_id, first_name, last_name, title, suffix, address, opt_line, city, state, zip, gifts, gift_total, address2, donor_name, city_state_zip).
notes: Alphanumeric fields must be wrapped in single quotes.
Empty fields are NULL not empty string ""; search on '' returns nothing.
Searching NULL is the same as searching '%'.
For different return fields, use a Dynamic SELECT instead.

dp_savedonor.returns: donor_id of created donor; for updates, returns 0.
notes: @donor_id=0 creates a new record; passing an existing donor_id updates it.
When updating, all specified fields OVERWRITE existing values.
Use two consecutive single quotes ('') inside strings to embed an apostrophe (O''Reilly).
Do not put spaces in the leading part of the URL or the API will report missing @donor_id.

parameters.name: @donor_id
parameters.notes: 0 for new; existing donor_id to update

parameters.name: @donor_type
parameters.notes: 'IN' for Individual or 'CO' for Corporate; configurable via DONOR_TYPE codes

parameters.name: @user_id
parameters.notes: Application name for auditing; not validated against DP users

dp_gifts.purpose: Return a predefined set of fields for all gifts given by a donor.
dp_gifts.returns: Records with gift_date2, amount, total, sub_solicit_code, campaign, balance, gl, solicit_code, reference, record_type, gift_id, donor_id, anongift, gift_aid_date.
notes: Use a Dynamic SELECT if you need different/additional fields.

dp_savegift.purpose: Create or update a gift, pledge payment, soft credit, split gift, or pledge payment.
notes: @gift_id=0 to create new; pass existing gift_id to update.
When @record_type='S' (soft credit), @glink must point to the actual gift's gift_id.
For split gifts: Main gift @record_type='M' @split_gift='N'; each split @record_type='G' @split_gift='Y' @glink=<main gift_id>; financial fields on the Main use literal 'SEE_SPLIT'.
For pledge payments: @pledge_payment='Y' and @plink=<parent pledge gift_id>.
Setting @nocalc='N' is required for the gift to appear on the reports dashboard.
DO NOT set code fields (@gl_code, @solicit_code, @sub_solicit_code, @campaign, @ty_letter_no) to '' — set to null when unused.
Do NOT include @first_gift — DP sets it automatically.
Fields recalculated by dp_savegift: @last_contrib_amt, @ytd, @ly_ytd, @gift_total, @gifts, @max_date, @max_amt, @avg_amt, @yrs_donated.

parameters.name: @record_type
parameters.notes: 'G' regular gift or split entry, 'P' pledge, 'M' main split header, 'S' soft credit, 'A' original (for adjustments), 'H' adjustment, 'C'/'J' adjusted-to-zero

parameters.name: @split_gift
parameters.notes: 'Y' for split children, 'N' for Main or non-split

parameters.name: @batch_no
parameters.notes: 0 or other batch number; will NOT accept NULL

parameters.name: @ty_letter_no
parameters.notes: Thank-you letter code; null when unused

parameters.name: @glink
parameters.notes: For split children: gift_id of Main. For soft credit (record_type='S'): gift_id of actual gift. null otherwise.

parameters.name: @plink
parameters.notes: For pledge payments: gift_id of parent pledge. null for non-recurring.

parameters.name: @nocalc
parameters.notes: Standard value 'N' so gifts appear in reports dashboard

parameters.name: @receipt_delivery_g
parameters.notes: N=do not acknowledge, E=email, B=email+letter, L=letter

parameters.name: @acknowledgepref
parameters.notes: Canadian only: '1AR' Acknowledge/Receipt, '2AD' Ack/no Receipt, '3DD' no Ack/no Receipt

dp_savepledge.purpose: Create or update a parent pledge (not pledge payments).
notes: Subsequent pledge payments are created with dp_savegift using record_type='G' and @plink=<pledge gift_id>.
For DP's EFT functionality to auto-process pledge payments, the parent pledge's DPGIFTUDF.EFT field must be 'Y' (set via dp_save_udf_xml).
Populate @vault_id with the SafeSave Vault ID for the pledge to be active.
If @currency not specified, defaults to USD.

parameters.name: @total
parameters.notes: Total pledged or 0 for ad infinitum

parameters.name: @frequency
parameters.notes: M monthly, Q quarterly, S semi-annually, A annually

parameters.name: @vault_id
parameters.notes: Required from SafeSave for active pledge

dp_savecontact.purpose: Save fields to DPCONTACT — creates or updates a Contact record for a donor.
notes: Codes for @activity_code, @mailing_code, @by_whom come from DPCODES / Code Maintenance.
@completed_date typically null — assigned user marks complete in UI.

dp_saveotherinfo.purpose: Save fields to DPTHERINFO — creates or updates an Other Info record for a donor.
dp_saveotherinfo.returns: other_id of saved record (used as @matching_id for DPTHERINFOUDF saves).

dp_saveaddress.purpose: Add or update a secondary/seasonal address (DPADDRESS table — appears in Address tab).
dp_saveaddress.returns: address_id of created or updated address.
notes: Seasonal dates use MMYY format (e.g., 1117 = November 2017).

dp_save_udf_xml.purpose: Save a single User Defined Field (UDF) value to any UDF table.
notes: @matching_id chooses which UDF table: donor_id => DPUDF; gift_id => DPGIFTUDF; contact_id => DPCONTACTUDF; other_id => DPTHERINFOUDF; address_id => DPADDRESSUDF.
Use only one of @char_value / @date_value / @number_value based on @data_type; set the others to null.
UDF field must already exist in your DP system (Code Maintenance > Field Definitions).
Quebec Law 25 fields (PM_*, PO_*) are stored as UDFs — use this call.

parameters.name: @data_type
parameters.notes: C Character, D Date, N Numeric

dp_savecode.purpose: Add or update a DPCODES drop-down value (GL codes, solicit codes, campaign codes, etc.).
dp_savecode.returns: 0 on create/update; '2' if @original_code=NULL and @code already exists (no-op).
notes: Behavior of @original_code:
- @original_code=@code AND code exists => UPDATE
- @original_code=NULL AND code exists => returns '2', no update
- @original_code=NULL OR @original_code=@code AND code does NOT exist => CREATE
Set @inactive='N' for codes to appear in UI dropdowns.
Many parameters can be NULL — typically supply only @field_name, @code, @description, @inactive, @user_id.

parameters.name: @original_code
parameters.notes: NULL for new; existing code for update

dp_savelink.purpose: Create a relationship link between two donors (DPLINK).
notes: Reciprocal links are auto-created — e.g., Parent link from A to B yields Child link from B to A.
@link_code comes from Code Maintenance > Link Type codes (e.g., 'FR' friend, 'PA' parent).

dp_savemultivalue_xml.purpose: Set a single checkbox value in DPUSERMULTIVALUES. Prefer mergemultivalues instead.
notes: Checkbox = checked => row exists in DPUSERMULTIVALUES with matching_id=donor_id. Unchecked => no row.
Use mergemultivalues to set multiple values or unset some — this call only sets a single value.

mergemultivalues.purpose: Replace the full set of checked values for a checkbox field — preferred way to set/unset checkboxes.
notes: Any CODE values not in @valuestring will be unset (unchecked).
Parameter names are different — @matchingid (no underscore), @fieldname, @valuestring.
List discoverable values: action=select code, description from dpcodes where field_name='<field>'.
List currently set values for a donor: action=select id, matching_id, code from dpusermultivalues where field_name='<field>' and matching_id=<donor_id>.

dp_deletemultivalues_xml.purpose: Delete ALL checked checkbox values on a screen tab for a donor. Prefer mergemultivalues.
notes: Recommended workflow: SELECT field_name, code from dpusermultivalues where field_name='<f>' and matching_id=<donor_id>; then delete; then re-save the ones you want to keep.

parameters.name: @table_name
parameters.notes: Screen tab: MAIN, GIFT, PLEDGE, BIO, OTHER

dp_saveflag_xml.purpose: Set a flag on a donor (DPFLAGS) — flags appear at the top of the Main tab.
notes: Flags are NOT the same as checkboxes — flags are in DPFLAGS, checkboxes in DPUSERMULTIVALUES.
Flag codes must be predefined in Settings > Code Maintenance.
Re-setting an existing flag returns a PRIMARY KEY violation error.
List flags for a donor: select * from dpflags where donor_id=<id>.

parameters.name: @donor_id
parameters.notes: or gift_id / other_id depending on target

parameters.name: @flag
parameters.notes: Code value from DPCODES (e.g., 'AL' for Alumni)

dp_delflags_xml.purpose: Delete ALL flags for a donor (DPFLAGS).
notes: Individual flag deletion is not currently supported — only all-or-nothing.
Workflow to keep some: SELECT * FROM dpflags WHERE donor_id=<id>; delete all; re-save desired ones via dp_saveflag_xml.

dp_tribAnon_MyTribSummary.purpose: Retrieve list of tributes (all or active-only).
dp_tribAnon_MyTribSummary.returns: Records with TributeID, ActiveFlg, Type, Name, Created, Gift_Amt, Gift_Count, Notif_Name.
notes: @userId must be null for API calls.

dp_tribAnon_Search.returns: Records with TributeID, Name, DPCode_ID, ActiveFlg, UserCreateDT, Created_Date, Legacy_ID, CodeDescription.
notes: Use the returned DPCode_ID for matching tribute type in dp_tribAnon_Create.

parameters.name: @Keywords
parameters.notes: All or part of tribute name

notes: Get @DPCodeID via: SELECT CODE, CODE_ID, DESCRIPTION FROM DPCODES WHERE FIELD_NAME='MEMORY_HONOR'. Common: M=In Memory Of, H=In Honor Of.
Run that query once per DP system — values are stable.
@Recipients accepts a single donor_id or pipe-delimited list ('11101|22202|33303') in single quotes.

parameters.name: @Recipients
parameters.notes: donor_id or pipe-delimited list in quotes. Optional — omit to create with no recipients.

dp_tribAnon_AssocTribsToGift.purpose: Associate a tribute (or comma-separated tributes) with a gift.
notes: Use dp_tribAnon_MyTribSummary to discover TributeID values.

dp_tribAnon_SaveTribRecipient.purpose: Add a tribute notification recipient for one specific gift.
notes: MUST use named parameter syntax (@this=1, @that=2,).
Follow with dp_tribNotif_Save to actually create the notification record.

dp_tribNotif_Save.purpose: Create the actual tribute gift notification record — runs AFTER dp_tribAnon_SaveTribRecipient.
notes: Notification shows as a new gift on the recipient with Type=Send Notification.
Appears in the Linked Gifts table of the original gift.
Most fields should mirror the original gift; key marker: @ty_letter_no='NT', @record_type='N', @gift_type='SN'.

parameters.name: @address_id
parameters.notes: 0 unless letter going to alternate address

dp_tribAnon_Update.purpose: Update tribute properties AND recipient list (affects ALL gifts on the tribute, not per-gift).
dp_tribAnon_Update.returns: Tribute record with TributeID, Name, ActiveFlg, UserCreateDt, DPCode_ID, CodeDescription, NotificantNames.
notes: IMPORTANT: Must include EXISTING recipients in @recipients or they will be removed.
Retrieve existing: SELECT r.tributeid, t.name, r.donor_id, r.createddate, t.activeflg FROM dptributesanon t, dptributesanon_recipients r WHERE t.tributeid=r.tributeid AND t.tributeid={id}.
@recipients prefixed with N and wrapped in single quotes: @recipients=N'105|43256|323387|137'.
Do NOT need to follow with dp_tribNotif_Save.

dp_PaymentMethod_Insert.purpose: Insert a DP Payment Method (DPPAYMENTMETHOD). EFT Transactions feature must be enabled.
notes: Normally populated by ecommerce integrations.
Requires SafeSave Customer Vault ID; create via SafeSave API first.
Once created, subsequent pledge payments can be processed through the DP UI without further API code.

dp_PaymentMethod_Update.returns: Full record with DpPaymentMethodID, donor_id, IsDefault, AccountType, dpPaymentMethodTypeID, CardNumberLastFour, CardExpirationDate, BankAccountNumberLastFour, NameOnAccount, CreatedDate, ModifiedDate, CustomerVaultID, import_id, created_by, modified_by, selected_currency, wl_import_id, notFoundonGateway, last_sync_date.
notes: Pre-step: SELECT * from dppaymentmethod where customervaultid=<id> — you MUST reload all existing values even if not changing them.
